title: Xerophilous Dutch Almond Butter Risotto
description: A unique and refreshing take on the classic risotto, incorporating the richness of almond butter in a shojin-friendly recipe. This dish features a light and velvety texture, perfect for a warm evening.

ingredients.name: Khao Khua
ingredients.notes: Dry-fry rice grains until lightly toasted, then grind into a fine powder.

ingredients.name: Almond Butter
ingredients.notes: Creamy almond butter is essential to this recipe, providing a rich and velvety texture.

ingredients.name: Vegetable Broth
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium broth is essential to avoid overpowering the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince garlic and saut9 until lightly fragrant.

ingredients.name: Shallot
ingredients.notes: Finely chop the shallot and saut9 until translucent.

ingredients.name: Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: A squeeze of fresh lemon juice adds brightness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Chopped Fresh Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh cilantro adds a pop of color and freshness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: A pinch of flaky sea salt enhances the flavors of the dish.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: A few grinds of black pepper add depth and warmth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: A drizzle of olive oil adds richness and moisture to the dish.

ingredients.name: Rice Flour
ingredients.notes: A small amount of rice flour helps thicken the dish.

instructions: Heat the vegetable broth in a separate pot and keep warm.
In a large saucepan, saut9 the garlic and shallot in olive oil until lightly fragrant.
Add the khao khua and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the almond butter and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the warm broth, stirring constantly and allowing each portion to absorb before adding the next.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 18-20 minutes, stirring frequently.
Stir in the lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Stir in the rice flour to thicken the dish.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
